summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates host secrets and filesystem data to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes shell commands that collect host identity (id, hostname, env vars via `env | sort`), read installer credentials (/root/.npmrc, /run/secrets, /var/run/secrets, /usr/etc/npmrc), enumerate filesystem locations (/root, /home, /opt, /tmp), scrape /proc/*/cmdline, and search for files matching *flag*, *secret*, *token*, *htb*, *.env. The output is base64-encoded and PUT to http://154.57.164.71:30782/api/modules/ECT-839201 over plain HTTP via http.request. Execution is gated by isChallengeWorker(), which requires a 12-hex Docker-style hostname, cwd containing /app/node_modules, and presence of /app/Cargo.toml or /app/package.json — evasion logic that hides the payload from dev laptops and Chinese-mirror installs while firing on CTF/CI sandbox targets. The harm satisfies the install-time, attacker-benefit gate: lifecycle execution on `npm install` ships installer credentials and filesystem contents to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
